---
title: "Workshop 03 - Data Analysis in R"
subtitle: "Tidyverse Workflows for Biodiversity Data"
author: "Maria Lopez"
date: 2024-09-01
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    footer: "ARBO-WATCH — Workshop 03"
---

## Overview

- Introduction to the tidyverse
- Cleaning and wrangling field data
- Visualising biodiversity patterns with ggplot2

---

## The Tidyverse

- A collection of R packages designed for data science
- Consistent grammar: `dplyr`, `tidyr`, `ggplot2`, `readr`
- Pipe-based workflows: `|>`

---

## Data Cleaning

```{.r}
df_clean <- df |>
  drop_na(species, count) |>
  filter(count > 0) |>
  mutate(date = as.Date(date))
```

- Remove rows with missing critical values
- Filter out invalid records
- Parse dates correctly

---

## Data Wrangling

```{.r}
species_summary <- df_clean |>
  group_by(species) |>
  summarise(
    total_count = sum(count),
    n_sites = n_distinct(site),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

- Aggregate counts per species
- Count number of distinct sites per species

---

## Visualisation

```{.r}
species_summary |>
  slice_max(total_count, n = 10) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(species, total_count), y = total_count)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2E7D32") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal()
```

---

## Summary

- Tidyverse provides a consistent, readable workflow
- Always inspect and clean data before analysis
- ggplot2 makes it easy to produce publication-quality figures
